name: Kolide API
what_it_does: Honest security platform built on osquery that surfaces endpoint compliance issues to end users via Slack. REST API for device health status, compliance checks, and integration with IdP/SSO for access control.
use_cases: Querying device health and compliance status for zero-trust access decisions
Automating compliance enforcement workflows that communicate with end users
Integrating endpoint posture data with Okta/Azure AD for conditional access
Building security dashboards showing device compliance trends
Automating onboarding checks before granting network access to new devices
not_for: Organizations without Slack (Kolide's primary UX channel is Slack-based)
Traditional EDR/blocking use cases — Kolide is transparency-focused
best_when: An agent needs structured device compliance data to make zero-trust access decisions or needs to surface device health transparently to end users.
avoid_when: You need active threat blocking or incident response — Kolide is compliance-focused, not prevention.

auth.notes: API keys from Kolide console. OAuth2 integration with Okta and Azure AD for device-to-identity binding. Webhook events for compliance state changes. Per-device trust signals exposed via API.

pricing.notes: Per-device monthly pricing. Kolide acquired by 1Password in 2023, expanding device trust capabilities. Pricing subject to change post-acquisition.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API documentation is limited compared to enterprise MDM vendors. Webhook integration is the primary event-driven interface. REST API covers device inventory and compliance state.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Device compliance state queries are inherently idempotent read operations.
known_agent_gotchas: Slack dependency: primary user communication is Slack-based, not API-driven
Device identity is tied to Kolide agent installation — enrollment required before data available
Post-1Password acquisition, API surface may change — check current documentation
Compliance check results may lag based on osquery schedule intervals

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II. TLS enforced. Built on osquery with read-only endpoint access. 1Password acquisition brings additional security credibility. Device trust signals cryptographically verified.
